tips: Take folic acid tablet every day — it protects your baby's brain and spine
Eat small meals often to help with nausea
Drink at least 8 cups of clean water every day
Eat iron-rich foods — beans, lentils, dark green vegetables
Avoid alcohol, raw meat, and soft cheese completely
Eat a little more food than usual — your baby is growing fast
Eat calcium foods every day — milk, yoghurt, small fish
Continue iron-rich foods to prevent tiredness and anaemia
Eat oranges and tomatoes — they help your body absorb iron
Sleep on your left side to improve blood flow to your baby
Eat smaller meals more often — your baby takes up a lot of space now
Eat more protein — eggs, beans, chicken, fish
Keep drinking water — dehydration can cause early contractions
Do not lie flat on your back for long periods
Pack your hospital bag from week 36 so you are ready
If you are breastfeeding, eat more food than usual every day
Drink plenty of water to help your body make milk
Eat iron-rich foods to recover from blood loss during birth
Rest whenever your baby sleeps — your recovery matters too
If you feel very sad or anxious for more than 2 weeks, tell your doctor
